hard.next_step: 10% confidence Apr 27 AI appetite. Larger AI initiative in Sinar Mas conglomerate. In parallel, check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo) Mar 24 Meeting with Head of Strategic Procurement Sinar Mas. Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend. Use cases to be shared by mid of April Mar 20 Can be part of a broader S2P opportunity at Sinar Mas conglomerate (SG/Indonesia) Mar 13: currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities. Demo to be scheduled by mid-April.
hard.ais_why: AI appetite tied to a Sinar Mas conglomerate-wide agentic-AI initiative and shareholder mandate; single-SKU ANA (autonomous negotiation) play alongside incumbent Coupa; no demo yet, data-centre localization gating, use cases pending.

north_star_verdict.headline: Technical interest is real but the deal has not moved since the 13 Mar introduction call: Calvin Sidharta never delivered the Anna use-cases or demo availability he committed to, there has been no PT SMART-facing activity in over 60 days, and no economic buyer, champion, or commercial conversation exists.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 16 Mar 2026, Qualified 22 Mar 2026, roughly 106 days in Qualified with no stage progression. Close date 1 Dec 2026 (about 148 days out). Last logged activity 4 May 2026 (63 days ago, a Sinarmas Mining intro call, not PT SMART). Last genuine PT SMART two-way exchange was the 13 Mar 2026 call, about 115 days ago. Buyer committed on 13 Mar to send use cases by email and to book a 30-45 minute Anna demo, neither delivered. Rep confidence logged at 10%. Cold beyond 60 days with committed deliverables undelivered forces Off Track.
evidence: Buyer expressed AI appetite tied to the S/4HANA migration (2027 staggered rollout) and a shareholder mandate to embed agentic AI into S2P (Calvin Sidharta, 13 Mar 2026)
Buyer runs Coupa as full S2P incumbent for about 1.5 years, direct and indirect (Calvin Sidharta, 15 Sep 2025 and 13 Mar 2026)

Buyer asked to understand Anna capabilities to build an internal case and requested a standalone Anna demo (Calvin Sidharta, 13 Mar 2026: 'Really understand about what Anna has to be able to make a case here internal')
Buyer committed to send detailed use cases by email and share demo availability; neither delivered in about 115 days
Zycus disclosed zero direct Indonesian Anna references (Guillaume Pasquet, 13 Mar 2026: 'we don't have direct local entities... we cannot fail with our first implementation in Indonesia')
No PT SMART-facing buyer touch since 13 Mar 2026; the only later activity is a 4 May 2026 Sinarmas Mining intro call (related conglomerate entity, no transcript)
Amount cut from $200k to $100k on 24 Apr 2026 and scope narrowed to the single ANA SKU (field history; Products__c = ANA)
No Economic Buyer engaged; a conglomerate AI mandate exists but the budget owner for PT SMART is unmapped
Owner reassigned to Carl Kimball on 1 Jul 2026 from Guillaume Pasquet (field history), an internal handover, not buyer movement

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated qualitative pain but never quantified it. Calvin Sidharta named material master-data quality as the trigger on 15 Sep 2025 ('we are currently facing some issues in regards to our master data, our material master... whether Zycus can help normalizing the data following the UNSPSC standard') and on 13 Mar 2026 framed the driver as a shareholder mandate to embed agentic AI into S2P alongside the S/4HANA transformation. No cost of the manual tail-spend or negotiation workload, no headcount or cycle-time target, and no ROI or savings model has been stated. Guillaume Pasquet pointed to tail-spend automation as the clear ROI case ('tail spend management, high volumes, low value... in term of ROI it's super clear', 13 Mar 2026) but the buyer never put numbers to it. The move is a tail-spend value-scoping session with Calvin and the continuous-improvement lead to size volume, manual touch-time, and negotiation cycles Anna would eliminate.
sources: Avoma call 13 Mar 2026 (Calvin Sidharta, Guillaume Pasquet)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged and none is confirmed for PT SMART. Calvin Sidharta (Decision Maker) said he needs to 'make a case here internal' (13 Mar 2026), which signals budget authority sits above him and is not yet identified. Ravinder Mawa carries a Chief Information Officer and Chief Procurement Officer title in the contact roles, but that record is tied to Sinarmas Mining (a sibling conglomerate entity) with no assigned role and no appearance on any PT SMART call, so treating him as the PT SMART budget owner is inference, not fact. The shareholder AI mandate implies a senior conglomerate sponsor exists but the person is unmapped. The move is to ask Calvin directly who owns the ANA budget for PT SMART versus the conglomerate track and to secure an introduction.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Ravinder Mawa, CIO & CPO, Sinarmas Mining, no role)
Next Step (Sinar Mas conglomerate AI initiative)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging but not formalized. From the calls the buyer is weighing AI maturity and native agentic architecture (Calvin asked what percentage of Zycus customers already run agentic AI and for similar-industry use cases, 13 Mar 2026), whether Anna can run standalone without displacing Coupa (Guillaume confirmed ANA is sold independently of the S2P suite, 13 Mar 2026), data localization and data flow (Guillaume flagged these as the demo questions to expect), and Indonesian reference proof (Calvin asked twice whether any Indonesian customer has implemented this). No formal RFP, BRD, or scored requirements document exists yet. The move is to convert these into a written evaluation checklist Calvin can take internally.
sources: Avoma call 13 Mar 2026 (Calvin Sidharta, Guillaume Pasquet)

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is undefined. Across two recorded calls the only consistent buyer participant is Calvin Sidharta (continuous-improvement and category lead), supported by an unverified team; no procurement approver, IT owner, or executive sponsor has joined. The stated next step has been the same for over 100 days (a 30-45 minute Anna demo with presales) and has not happened, so no evaluation stages, approval chain, or timeline are visible. Calvin explicitly said any move requires him to build an internal case first (13 Mar 2026). The process must be mapped: named stages, who signs off on new procurement software, and how the conglomerate AI initiative governs a PT SMART purchase.
sources: Avoma calls 15 Sep 2025 and 13 Mar 2026 (call participation sequence)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in view. The deal is at Qualified with no commercial proposal, no MSA or Order Form discussion, and no legal, InfoSec, or procurement-onboarding steps started. Delivery would involve BCT as the local Indonesian partner alongside the Zycus delivery team for an ANA standalone implementation (Guillaume Pasquet, 13 Mar 2026: 'for iNAS standalone... you will need our own Zycus delivery team to be involved in parallel of partners like BCT'). A likely gate is Indonesian data-centre localization, flagged as mandatory in the rep notes. Paper-process visibility only becomes meaningful after a demo and a scoped pilot; there is nothing to redline today.
sources: AIS_Why note (DC local requirements mandatory for Indonesia)

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain is real but modest and not yet urgent. Calvin Sidharta owns two confirmed pains: master-data and material-master quality ahead of the S/4HANA cutover ('we don't want garbage in, garbage out', 15 Sep 2025) and a shareholder push to embed agentic AI into S2P processes (13 Mar 2026). Inferred pain, per Guillaume's positioning, is manual, high-volume, low-value tail-spend negotiation that Anna automates. The pain owner is Calvin, but because Coupa already covers full S2P the pain is additive (a new AI capability) rather than a burning replacement need, which is why urgency is low. Quantifying the tail-spend workload is the lever that turns this from curiosity into a funded project.
sources: Avoma call 13 Mar 2026 (Calvin Sidharta, Guillaume Pasquet)

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Calvin Sidharta is receptive and technically engaged (he drove both calls, asked detailed questions, and said 'I think we can move forward here with this', 13 Mar 2026) but he is not advocating: he committed to send use cases and demo availability and delivered neither in about 115 days, has not re-engaged since, and has not introduced a budget owner or created internal urgency. That is interest, not sponsorship. Lily Wawolumaja (Head of Strategic Procurement, Evaluator) was named as a meeting target on 24 Mar but has not appeared on a recorded call. Building Calvin into an active champion, or finding a more senior sponsor tied to the conglomerate AI mandate, is the prerequisite to any progress.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Calvin Sidharta, Decision Maker; Lily Wawolumaja, Evaluator)

competition.narrative: Coupa is the entrenched incumbent, running full S2P (direct and indirect) for about 1.5 years, confirmed by Calvin Sidharta on 15 Sep 2025 and 13 Mar 2026. Crucially Zycus is NOT trying to displace Coupa: Guillaume Pasquet positioned ANA as a standalone autonomous-negotiation module sold independently of the S2P suite ('this ANA module is available standalone... we are selling it independently', 13 Mar 2026), and confirmed intake and the wider suite cannot integrate on top of Coupa. So the real contest is not Zycus versus Coupa on S2P; it is Anna versus the status quo of doing nothing new, with AI maturity as the wedge (Guillaume: 'the market clearly recognizes Zycus is more mature in AI than any other vendor including Coupa'). The dominant current threat is inertia: the buyer is reasonably satisfied with Coupa, only 1.5 years into that contract, and has no compelling event forcing an ANA decision.
sources: Avoma call 13 Mar 2026 (Calvin Sidharta, Guillaume Pasquet)

critical_signals.text: The real fight is not against Coupa but against doing nothing: PT SMART is only 1.5 years into a full Coupa S2P deployment and Zycus is layering Anna alongside it, not replacing it.
The 13 March introduction call ended with Calvin Sidharta asking for an Anna demo and committing to send use cases, but neither has materialized in over three months.
The amount was cut from $200k to $100k on 24 April and scope narrowed to the single Anna module, with no proposal, pricing, or ROI case yet on the table.
No economic buyer is engaged; Calvin Sidharta says he must build the case internally, and the conglomerate AI-mandate sponsor remains unnamed and never met.

deal_movement.summary: The deal has been effectively static since the 13 Mar 2026 introduction call. The only later touches are internal: an amount cut on 24 Apr, an unrelated Sinarmas Mining intro call on 4 May, and an owner reassignment to Carl Kimball on 1 Jul. No PT SMART buyer has advanced a next step in over 100 days.

items.change: Owner reassigned from Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball (internal handover)
Sinarmas Mining introductory call logged (related conglomerate entity, no transcript)
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is inertia / do-nothing, not a rival vendor: PT SMART is satisfied enough with incumbent Coupa (full S2P, direct and indirect, about 1.5 years in) that there is no compelling event to buy Anna now. Coupa is the incumbent but is NOT the head-to-head competitor here because Zycus is selling Anna standalone alongside Coupa rather than displacing it (Guillaume Pasquet, 13 Mar 2026). AI maturity is the Zycus wedge, but with no quantified case, no economic buyer, and a 1.5-year-old Coupa contract, status quo wins by default unless a tail-spend ROI case and a decision timeline are created.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Defer
competitors.quote: Really understand about what Anna has to be able to make a case here internal. But I do understand. It doesn't have to be today (Calvin Sidharta, 13 Mar 2026)
competitors.source: Avoma call 13 Mar 2026; SF LastActivityDate 4 May 2026; 10% rep confidence
competitors.how_we_win: Manufacture a compelling event by tying an Anna tail-spend pilot to the S/4HANA 2027 cutover and the shareholder AI-readiness mandate, and get Calvin to name the budget owner and a decision date.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: We are using Coupa already... it's been one and a half years... full S2P, direct and indirect (Calvin Sidharta, 15 Sep 2025 and 13 Mar 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Do not fight the Coupa S2P displacement battle; win the additive Anna autonomous-negotiation slot on AI maturity (20 years, native agentic architecture, 50+ Anna customers) that Coupa's bolt-on AI cannot match, proven in a standalone demo.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Anna fits the buyer's stated AI ambition but the buyer needs local proof and a standalone path that does not disrupt Coupa. Zycus meets the standalone requirement but is exposed on Indonesian references and on a quantified case.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, single-threaded, early-stage Anna standalone play against an entrenched Coupa incumbent with no compelling event. The win path is to create urgency around a quantified tail-spend pilot, break out of the single Calvin thread to a budget owner, and use the local BCT partner to reactivate.
flags: Re-open the relationship: the deal has had no PT SMART buyer touch in over 100 days, so the highest-impact move is Calvin re-engagement before anything else.

Multi-thread beyond Calvin Sidharta to a conglomerate AI-mandate sponsor and to procurement leadership (Lily Wawolumaja), since Calvin admits he must build the case internally and cannot fund it alone.
Attack inertia, not Coupa: build a quantified tail-spend ROI case tied to the S/4HANA 2027 cutover so Anna becomes a funded initiative rather than an optional add-on.

items.risk: If he owns the AI-initiative budget and is bypassed, the deal cannot be funded; if he is siloed to Sinarmas Mining he may be noise. Must be clarified with Calvin.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Calvin Sidharta (Decision Maker) is receptive and technically curious but has not advocated: he drove both calls and voiced interest ('I think we can move forward here with this', 13 Mar 2026), yet failed to deliver the use cases and demo availability he committed to about 115 days ago, has not re-engaged since, and has introduced no budget owner or internal urgency. That is interest, not sponsorship. Without an advocate who will carry Anna to a funded decision, the deal cannot progress past exploration.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (no Champion role); Avoma calls 15 Sep 2025 and 13 Mar 2026; SF LastActivityDate 4 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led with a strong agentic-AI narrative (Merlin, I2O intake-to-outcomes, autonomous negotiation for tail spend) and the buyer explicitly came for AI maturity ('that is why we are having this call'). But the narrative has not converted: no demo, no quantified use case, and Anna's autonomous-negotiation value has not been proven live to PT SMART.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate is consistent with the calls: genuine buyer-stated AI appetite tied to a shareholder agentic-AI mandate and the S/4HANA transformation, but tempered by the lack of a demo, no quantified case, and a duplicated NextStep shared with the sibling Sinarmas opportunity. Real interest, unproven and un-progressed.

items.detail: No economic buyer engaged; Calvin admits he must build the case internally and no conglomerate AI-mandate sponsor is mapped.
Amount cut from $200k to $100k on 24 Apr and scope narrowed to the single Anna SKU, signaling a defensive, phased, low-cost appetite.
No direct Indonesian Anna reference exists, and the buyer asked for one twice; first-in-market risk that Zycus itself flagged ('we cannot fail with our first implementation in Indonesia').
Coupa is a satisfied 1.5-year incumbent with no renewal pressure, so adding Anna is optional and easily deferred (do-nothing risk).

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has gone cold: no PT SMART buyer touch since 13 Mar, committed deliverables undelivered, 10% rep confidence, and an internal owner change on 1 Jul. The relationship needs re-opening before any milestone can be planned.
items: No buyer-initiated contact in over 100 days
Buyer commitments (use cases, demo availability) undelivered since 13 Mar 2026
Owner reassigned to Carl Kimball on 1 Jul 2026, requiring a fresh introduction

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A cold, single-threaded Anna standalone play into a satisfied Coupa account: the buyer showed real AI curiosity on 13 Mar but never delivered the use cases or demo availability he promised, there is no economic buyer, no quantified case, and no PT SMART touch in over 100 days, so the only path is to re-open the relationship and manufacture urgency around a tail-spend pilot.

win_position.text: This is an early, un-progressed deal against an entrenched incumbent: PT SMART runs Coupa full S2P (1.5 years in) and Zycus is adding Anna alongside it, not displacing it, with no compelling event to buy now.
Genuine AI appetite exists: Calvin Sidharta cited a shareholder mandate to embed agentic AI into S2P and came specifically for Zycus AI maturity ('that is why we are having this call', 13 Mar 2026).
No economic buyer is engaged and no champion is advocating: Calvin admits he must build the case internally and delivered none of his 13 Mar commitments in about 115 days.
Zycus has zero direct Indonesian Anna references, which it flagged as a make-or-break first implementation, and the buyer asked for a local reference twice.

deal_momentum.text: No PT SMART buyer-facing activity since the 13 Mar 2026 call; the promised Anna demo has never been scheduled.
Engagement is still pre-demo and generic; the buyer has not requested the next meeting or named any date.
The only recent movements are internal (amount cut 24 Apr, owner change 1 Jul), not buyer progression.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer owes and has not delivered the detailed use cases and demo availability he committed to on 13 Mar 2026.
Amount was cut from $200k to $100k and scope narrowed to a single module, signaling low, defensive commitment.
Calvin remained personally engaged across two calls over six months and explicitly wants to understand Anna deeply to justify it internally.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing is the dominant threat: a satisfied 1.5-year Coupa incumbent with no renewal pressure makes an Anna add-on easy to defer indefinitely.
Single-threaded on Calvin with no mapped budget owner; if he stays passive there is no one to fund or drive the decision.
First-in-market Indonesian implementation risk with no local reference, which Zycus itself called a deal it 'cannot fail'.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; Calvin is receptive but passive and delivered none of his commitments (13 Mar 2026).

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB engaged; Calvin must build the case internally and no budget owner is mapped.

momentum.evidence: No buyer touch in over 100 days; promised demo never scheduled.

scope_change.from: $200k, positioned as part of a broader S2P opportunity across the Sinar Mas conglomerate
scope_change.to: $100k, single ANA (autonomous negotiation) module sold standalone
scope_change.detail: Amount was halved on 24 Apr 2026 (field history) and the product scope is the single ANA SKU, down from the broader S2P framing in the 20 Mar next step. The buyer appears to be de-risking toward a small, optional, phased add-on rather than a suite decision.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer is exploring Anna for AI maturity but stays committed to Coupa for S2P and says a decision 'doesn't have to be today'; no Zycus-specific commercial next step taken (Calvin Sidharta, 13 Mar 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (master-data quality, shareholder AI mandate, tail-spend automation potential); no ROI, savings, or volume numbers stated by the buyer.

calls_omitted.reason: No transcript available (related conglomerate entity, not PT SMART)
